query: Thanks for the details! Yes, please go ahead and change the reservation as discussed. Use the certificates first, then the gift cards, and charge the rest to my master card. Just to confirm, after applying all my certificates and gift cards, my master card will be charged $1286, correct?

state.history_summary:
assistant: Hi! How can I help you today?
user: The user asks to check total gift-card and certificate balances and also change the most recent reservation to the cheapest business-class round trip on the same dates.
assistant: The assistant asks for the user ID and reservation ID.
user: The user provides user ID mohamed_silva_9265.
assistant: The assistant retrieves the user profile and most recent reservation, summarizes balances and the existing itinerary, searches same-date business-class options, calculates the total trip cost, and explains that using all certificates and gift cards would leave $1286 charged to the Mastercard.
user: The user asks for separate totals for gift-card and certificate balances.
assistant: The assistant provides the separate totals.
user: The user then confirms the reservation change, says to use certificates first and then gift cards, and asks whether the Mastercard charge will be $1286.

candidate_answers.text: Yes — based on the totals we just reviewed, the remaining charge would be $1286 after applying your certificates and gift cards. I can go ahead and process the reservation change using that payment order.
The itinerary, cabin upgrade, and payment order are already grounded in the visible context, so the next action is to update the reservation flights rather than recalculate or ask for more details.

provenance.source_type: manual annotation from real tau2 episode
provenance.decision_rule: Replace the invalid prepared index with the later user turn that explicitly confirms the business-class change and payment order after receiving the balance breakdown, because that is the real grounded decision point for applying the reservation update.
provenance.scoring_method: Manual one-step proxy scoring from the decision-point state. Scores reflect which action most directly advances the user's request using only visible conversation evidence.
notes: query comes directly from the selected user message, not from task hidden fields
history_summary stops before the later final confirmation turn and the actual update tool call
candidate_answers are manual non-leaky drafts grounded only in visible context
prepared tuple index 11 was invalid because it pointed to an assistant message, so this sample uses user index 22 instead
